summary: (2025*)This class will give students experience in giving the kinds of Scientific and Technical Academic English presentations that students may expect in their professional life. We will explore the most common information structures used to convey technical information, the proper use of data and graphics, and a common rhetorical structure used to organize data in both presentations and academic papers.
aims: (2025*)This four-skills course is designed to help you organize and express complex scientific and technical information in clear and simple English. One of the most difficult aspects of working in science and technology is communicating with people outside the field. However, learning to communicate complex ideas in the simplest language possible can be very important. There are many historical examples where an inability to communicate complex ideas has caused serious problems in society. Though we are using the medium of English, the communication tools and ideas presented in this class are useful in any language, even in your native language.
goals: (2025*)Students will be able to convey complex technical data in English using basic information structures.
(2025*)Students will be able to create appropriate academic English language presentations.
(2025*)Students will be able to engage in simple, informal conversations which are key to building professional international relationships.

plan.text: (2025*)Classroom Survey & Data Analysis / Presentation 3 Workshop. In this class, you and your group will conduct your class survey. This will be a bit awkward online, but I think we can manage.
(2025*)Information Structure: Situation, Problem, Solution, Evaluation / Presentation 4 Assignment
(2025*)Presentation 4 Workshop (Note: depending on class numbers, individual presentations may begin in this week.)

textbooks: (2025*)All materials used in class are my original materials. No additional textbook is required.
prereq: (2025*)This class is for Science students who should have completed the first year English requirement. While advanced English is not required, students should be prepared to communicate with the instructor primarily in English.
prep: (2025*)An effective presentation does not require perfect English. However, it does require significant rehearsal. Therefore, students will be required to spend enough time outside of class to ensure that their presentations are smooth and confident. In addition to developing the content and the graphics for each presentation, students must rehearse their English by using a voice-to-text application on their phones or computers. (This technique will be explained in detail in class.)
